revision.comment: 愛媛県の神社
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=多伎神社| 所在地=愛媛県今治市古谷乙47| 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=不詳| 本殿=流造| 例祭=[[5月15日]]}} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷（[[旧朝倉村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売]]・多伎都比古・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|鐃速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を授った。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 [[Category:愛媛県の神社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:県社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:今治市|たきしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=多伎神社| 所在地=愛媛県今治市古谷乙47| 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=不詳| 本殿=流造| 例祭=[[5月15日]]}} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷（[[旧朝倉村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売命]]・多伎都比古命・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|鐃速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を授った。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 [[Category:愛媛県の神社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:府県社|たきしんしや]] [[Category:今治市|たきしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

須佐之男命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・県社| 創建=不詳| 本殿=流造| 例祭=[[5月5日]]}} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売命]]・多伎都比古命・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を授った。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | native_name = 多伎神社 | image = 多伎神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | coordinates = {{coord|34.003529|132.994794}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | native_name = 多伎神社 | image = 多伎神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | coordinates = {{coord|34.003529|132.994794}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | native_name = 多伎神社 | image = 多伎神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | coordinates = {{coord|34.003529|132.994794}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]].

[[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|多伎神社|version=107359063|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=多伎神社（今治市） | 画像=[[File:多伎神社.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷乙47番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（名神大）・旧[[県社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[5月5日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=多伎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売命]]・多伎都比古命・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を賜り、[[延喜式]]では[[名神大社]]に列せられた。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] ==End Japanese==

[[ja:多伎神社]] {{Expand Japanese|多伎神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | native_name = 多伎神社 | image = 多伎神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Taki Shrine (Q11430618) | coordinates = {{coord|34.003529|132.994794}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Taki Shrine'''|多伎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|多伎神社|version=107359063|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=多伎神社（今治市） | 画像=[[File:多伎神社.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷乙47番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（名神大）・旧[[県社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[5月5日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=多伎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売命]]・多伎都比古命・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を賜り、[[延喜式]]では[[名神大社]]に列せられた。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|多伎神社|version=107359063|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=多伎神社（今治市） | 画像=[[File:多伎神社.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷乙47番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（名神大）・旧[[県社]] | 創建=不詳 | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[5月5日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=多伎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]古谷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は[[タギツヒメ|多伎都比売命]]・多伎都比古命・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]である。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる[[磐座]]があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『[[三代実録]]』[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（[[870年]]）には正四位上を賜り、[[延喜式]]では[[名神大社]]に列せられた。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]]

{{Infobox religious building

address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛媛県今治市古谷乙47番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多伎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430618}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=多伎神社（今治市） | 画像=[[File:多伎神社.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|愛媛県|Q123376}}{{jalink|今治市|Q461020}}古谷乙47番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（名神大）・旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建=不詳 | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|5月5日|Q2550}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=多伎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛媛県|Q123376}}{{jalink|今治市|Q461020}}古谷に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は{{jalink|タギツヒメ|Q10932592|多伎都比売命}}・多伎都比古命・{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}である。 === 概要 === 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる{{jalink|磐座|Q11587880}}があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『{{jalink|三代実録}}』{{jalink|貞観_(日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}2年（{{jalink|860年|Q24714}}）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（{{jalink|870年|Q23629}}）には正四位上を賜り、{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}では{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列せられた。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛媛県今治市古谷乙47番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多伎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430618}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=多伎神社（今治市） | 画像=[[File:多伎神社.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|愛媛県|Q123376}}{{jalink|今治市|Q461020}}古谷乙47番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=多伎都比売命
多伎都比古命
須佐之男命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（名神大）・旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建=不詳 | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|5月5日|Q2550}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=多伎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多伎神社'''（たきじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛媛県|Q123376}}{{jalink|今治市|Q461020}}古谷に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。「瀧の宮（多伎宮）」とも呼ばれ、伊予国内神名帳には「正一位　多伎不断大願大菩薩」とある。 祭神は{{jalink|タギツヒメ|Q10932592|多伎都比売命}}・多伎都比古命・{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}である。 === 概要 === 創建年代は不詳。社伝によれば、崇神天皇の御代、{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}六代の孫である伊香武雄命が「瀧の宮」の社号を奉り、初代斎宮になったという（社頭掲示板による）。 社殿は頓田川の支流・多伎川の畔にあり、周囲には30基余りの古墳が群集する（県指定史跡「多伎神社古墳群」）。多伎川を遡った山頂近くに川上巌（かわかみのいわお）と呼ばれる{{jalink|磐座|Q11587880}}があり、奥の院とされる。雨乞いに霊験があると伝えられ、もとはこの磐座信仰に始まると考えられる。 『{{jalink|三代実録}}』{{jalink|貞観_(日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}2年（{{jalink|860年|Q24714}}）に「伊豫国従五位上'''瀧神'''に従四位下を授ける」とあるのが文献上の初出である。以後累進して同12年（{{jalink|870年|Q23629}}）には正四位上を賜り、{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}では{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列せられた。 江戸時代には今治藩の祈願所となり、雨乞い祈願が行われた。祈願の際には藩主が参拝し、七日間の祈願の前半は本殿で、後半は川上巌で行われたという。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}{{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{jalink|Kensha}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Furuya, {{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}}, {{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Kensha}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{jalink|iwakura|Q11587880}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{jalink|870|Q23629}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} it was listed as a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taki Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:need translation]]

At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taki Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}{{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{jalink|Kensha}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Furuya, {{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}}, {{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Kensha}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{jalink|iwakura|Q11587880}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{jalink|870|Q23629}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} it was listed as a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taki Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}{{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{jalink|Kensha}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Furuya, {{jalink|Imabari City|Q461020}}, {{jalink|Ehime Prefecture|Q123376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Kensha}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{jalink|iwakura|Q11587880}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{jalink|Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{jalink|870|Q23629}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} it was listed as a {{jalink|Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taki Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]]

It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Taki Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{Shrine | Name=Taki Shrine (Imabari City) | Image=[[File:Taki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{Shrine | Name=Taki Shrine (Imabari City) | Image=[[File:Taki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名たきしんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{Shrine | Name=Taki Shrine (Imabari City) | Image=[[File:Taki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

{{Shrine | Name=Taki Shrine (Imabari City) | Image=[[File:Taki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

{{Shrine | Name=Taki Shrine (Imabari City) | Image=[[File:Taki Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} Furuya Otsu 47 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Susanoo-no-Mikoto | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Taki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Taki Shrine''' (たきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuya, {{ill|lt=Imabari City|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as “Taki-no-Miya (Taki Shrine),” and in the Iyo Province Deities Register it is listed as “Senior First Rank: Taki Fudan Daigan Dai Bosatsu.” The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}}, Tagitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. According to shrine tradition, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, Ikatakeo-no-Mikoto, a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}, dedicated the shrine name “Taki-no-Miya” and became the first saigū (chief ritual officiant) (according to the shrine notice board). The shrine buildings stand beside the Taki River, a tributary of the Tonda River, and more than 30 kofun (ancient burial mounds) are clustered in the surrounding area (the prefecturally designated historic site “Taki Shrine Kofun Group”). Near the summit upstream along the Taki River is a sacred rock called Kawakami no Iwao, regarded as the inner sanctuary; it is a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|WD=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}. It is said to have divine efficacy for rainmaking, and the shrine is thought to have originated in worship centered on this sacred rock. The earliest documentary reference appears in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}), which states: “The deity ‘Taki-no-Kami’ of Iyo Province, Junior Fifth Rank Upper, is granted Junior Fourth Rank Lower.” Thereafter the rank was successively promoted, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) it was granted Senior Fourth Rank Upper; in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. During the Edo period, it served as a prayer shrine of the Imabari Domain, where rainmaking prayers were performed. At the time of such prayers, the domain lord would worship at the shrine; the first half of the seven-day prayer period was conducted at the main hall, and the latter half at Kawakami no Iwao. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taki Shrine (Imabari)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:今治藩]]
